source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Handout C. SMART Goal Progress Tracker.pdf
output_file: 4-5.FL.2 Handout C. SMART Goal Progress Tracker.md

analysis.summary: This is a handout for grades 4-5 students designed to help them track progress on SMART goals as part of a financial literacy lesson. It includes a progress tracking table and reflection questions to assess goal achievement and learning outcomes.

entities.name: SMART Goal Progress Tracker
entities.description: A structured handout for tracking progress on SMART goals with reflection components

entities.name: Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy Lesson 2
entities.description: The parent lesson that this handout supports, focusing on financial literacy for elementary students

entities.name: SMART Goals
entities.description: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound goal-setting framework

entities.name: Progress Tracking
entities.description: The ability to monitor and document steps toward goal completion

entities.name: Self-Reflection
entities.description: The practice of evaluating one's own learning and goal achievement

entities.name: Goal Achievement Assessment
entities.description: Evaluation of whether SMART goals were successfully completed

entities.name: Learning Transfer
entities.description: Applying knowledge and experiences to future situations

relationships.context: This tracker is Handout C within the larger financial literacy lesson
The tracker is specifically designed to monitor SMART goals
Students track their progress on completing SMART goals
Reflection questions help students assess whether they achieved their goals
Students must first assess their achievement before applying learning to future situations
The handout includes reflection questions to develop self-assessment skills
